title: Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA)
description: Identity-based access to specific applications — replaces VPN with granular, context-aware access control.
fullExplanation: ZTNA (Zero Trust Network Access) provides secure remote access to specific applications based on user identity, device health, and context — without granting access to the entire network. Unlike VPN which creates a network tunnel giving access to everything on the network, ZTNA grants access only to the specific application the user needs. The user never touches the network — they connect to an application proxy that enforces policy.

ztnaVsVpn.ztna: Access only to specific authorized applications
Never trust, always verify — identity + device + context

ztnaVsVpn.vpn: Possible — attacker can reach all network
ztnaVsVpn.ztna: Prevented — no network access, only app access
Full device posture check before access

vendors.description: ZTNA as part of Cloudflare Zero Trust platform
ZTNA + CASB + SWG in one platform
Google's internal ZTNA — first large-scale implementation
WireGuard-based mesh VPN with ZTNA-like access controls

keyFacts: ZTNA replaces VPN for application access — not for site-to-site connectivity
Device posture: check OS version, patch level, EDR status before granting access
Continuous verification: re-evaluate access on each request, not just at login
Application connector: lightweight agent in DMZ/cloud connects app to ZTNA broker
No inbound firewall rules needed — app connector initiates outbound connection to broker
SASE (Secure Access Service Edge): ZTNA + SWG + CASB + SD-WAN in cloud-delivered platform
